clauses.title: Outsourcing register of relevant services
clauses.text: A bank must maintain a register of all its arrangements for the outsourcing of relevant services, including details of the service provider, the service outsourced, and whether it is treated as material.

clauses.title: Materiality assessment (MOORS)
clauses.text: A bank must assess and classify each outsourced relevant service to determine whether it is a Material Outsourced Relevant Service (MOORS) based on potential impact to operations, reputation, financial condition, and ability to comply with laws.

clauses.title: Notification of adverse developments
clauses.text: A bank must notify MAS no later than 14 working days of any adverse development arising from a MOORS (e.g. breach, severe service disruption).

clauses.title: Audit and inspection rights (Notice 658)
clauses.text: The bank, its agents (incl. external auditors), and MAS must be able to access and inspect the service provider, including documentation, records and premises.

clauses.title: Sound risk management of outsourced services
clauses.text: A bank must have a robust framework with board/senior management oversight to manage outsourcing risks across the lifecycle.

clauses.title: Board/senior management responsibility
clauses.text: Board and senior management institute the outsourcing risk framework and retain ultimate accountability even where functions are outsourced.

clauses.title: Due diligence on service provider
clauses.text: Conduct due diligence on financial soundness, technical/industry expertise, operational capability, reputation, security and internal controls before engaging.

clauses.title: Periodic re-due-diligence (<=24 months)
clauses.text: Perform periodic re-assessment of service providers for material arrangements at least once every 24 months.

clauses.title: Mandatory outsourcing agreement terms
clauses.text: A written, legally binding agreement must address scope, SLAs, confidentiality/security, audit rights, sub-contracting, BCM, default/termination, dispute resolution, governing law and liabilities.

clauses.title: Independent audit every <=3 years
clauses.text: Material outsourcing must be subject to independent audit/expert assessment of provider controls at least every 3 years.

clauses.title: Sub-contracting controls and approval
clauses.text: Agreements must address sub-contracting; the provider remains accountable; material sub-contracting needs the institution's prior knowledge/consent and equivalent controls.

clauses.title: Customer consent/disclosure for customer info
clauses.text: Where outsourcing discloses customer information, ensure compliance with secrecy obligations and obtain consent or a permitted basis.
clauses.source: MAS Guidelines on Outsourcing / Banking Act

clauses.title: Confidentiality & data segregation
clauses.text: Provider must protect confidentiality/integrity of bank/customer data, restrict access need-to-know, and isolate the bank's data in multi-tenant environments.

clauses.title: Cross-border outsourcing risk
clauses.text: For overseas processing, assess country/legal risk and ensure foreign law does not impede access by the bank or MAS and Singapore compliance is met.

clauses.title: Termination and exit strategy
clauses.text: Documented exit/contingency strategy enabling orderly transfer/in-sourcing, data return/destruction, and continuity during transition.

clauses.title: Business continuity of outsourced functions
clauses.text: Provider must have robust, tested BCP for outsourced functions aligned to the bank's recovery objectives.

clauses.title: Ongoing monitoring of provider
clauses.text: Continuously monitor performance, service levels, control effectiveness and risk profile across the lifecycle.

clauses.title: Outsourcing concentration risk
clauses.text: Assess and manage concentration from over-reliance on a single provider, shared providers, or geographic concentration.

clauses.title: Technology risk governance
clauses.text: Board/senior management establish technology risk governance, defined roles, a risk function, and an approved tech risk appetite.

clauses.title: Third-party technology vendor risk
clauses.text: Evaluate and manage technology risks of third-party vendors, including security posture, due diligence and contractual security/assurance.

clauses.title: Secure third-party/API connectivity
clauses.text: Strong controls over API/system connectivity: authentication, encryption in transit, input validation, and API monitoring.

clauses.title: Access control & PAM
clauses.text: Least-privilege/need-to-have access, strong auth, segregation of duties, and strict control/monitoring/review of privileged accounts.

clauses.title: Cryptography & key management
clauses.text: Protect data with strong cryptography and a robust key lifecycle (generation, storage, rotation, destruction).

clauses.title: Data & infrastructure security
clauses.text: Protect data at rest/in transit, harden infrastructure, prevent data loss, securely dispose of media, maintain classified asset inventory.

clauses.title: Cyber security operations & detection
clauses.text: Continuously monitor, detect and respond to threats with logging, correlation/analytics and threat intelligence.

clauses.title: Cyber incident response
clauses.text: Establish and test an incident response plan to contain, eradicate, recover and report incidents, including third-party-originated ones.

clauses.title: Vulnerability assessment & pen testing
clauses.text: Conduct regular VA/PT of systems (incl. third-party/internet-facing) and remediate vulnerabilities risk-based.

clauses.title: Adversarial attack simulation
clauses.text: Conduct scenario-based red-team exercises to validate defence/detection/response.

clauses.title: Secure SDLC
clauses.text: Adopt secure SDLC (secure coding, security requirements, security testing) for in-house and third-party software.

clauses.title: Source code review of vendor software
clauses.text: Perform source code review (or equivalent assurance) of vendor-supplied software to find vulnerabilities/malicious code before deployment.

clauses.title: Patch management
clauses.text: Identify, prioritise, test and apply security patches in a timely, risk-commensurate manner.

clauses.title: IT audit & independent assurance
clauses.text: Subject technology risk management, incl. outsourced services, to regular independent IT audit.

clauses.title: Securing administrative accounts
clauses.text: Secure every administrative account on OS/database to prevent unauthorised access; do not use for non-admin activity.

clauses.title: Application of security patches
clauses.text: Apply security patches to known vulnerabilities, or equivalent measures where patches are unavailable.

clauses.title: Security standards & hardening
clauses.text: Implement written security standards for every system, ensure conformance, and harden/securely configure systems.

clauses.title: Network perimeter defence
clauses.text: Implement perimeter controls to restrict unauthorised traffic and detect/block malicious access.

clauses.title: Malware protection
clauses.text: Implement malware protection on every system where available.

clauses.title: Multi-factor authentication
clauses.text: MFA for all admin accounts on critical systems and for systems accessing customer information via the internet.

clauses.title: Third-party dependency mapping
clauses.text: Identify/map dependencies on third parties (and sub-contractors) supporting critical business services to find single points of failure.

clauses.title: Service Recovery Time Objective
clauses.text: Establish an SRTO per critical business service and ensure third parties can meet it during disruptions.

clauses.title: Contractual recovery commitments
clauses.text: Contracts with third parties supporting critical services must include recovery/continuity obligations consistent with the bank's objectives.

clauses.title: Third-party BCP testing
clauses.text: Third-party BCPs for critical services must be tested (incl. joint exercises) and results reviewed.

clauses.title: Resilience concentration risk
clauses.text: Assess concentration where multiple critical services rely on the same third party, infrastructure or location.

clauses.title: Data location disclosure
clauses.text: Identify and disclose location(s) of cross-border processing/storage of bank/customer data, including by sub-contractors.

clauses.title: Enforceable cross-border audit access
clauses.text: Ensure bank/auditor/MAS audit and inspection rights remain legally enforceable for overseas data and are not impeded by foreign law.

clauses.title: Encryption of cross-border data
clauses.text: Customer/sensitive data transferred or stored overseas must be strongly encrypted in transit and at rest.

clauses.title: PDPA cross-border transfer
clauses.text: Personal data transferred outside Singapore must be protected by legally enforceable obligations comparable to the PDPA.
